title: Add user authentication
description: Introduces user accounts, login, session management, and role-based access control.

sections.heading: Documentation: what this PR does
sections.rationale: Start with the docs to understand the big picture before diving into implementation.

annotations.body: The 24-hour session expiry is hardcoded in `auth.ts`. Consider making this configurable.

sections.heading: Database schema: users and sessions tables
sections.rationale: The data model is the foundation — understand the tables before seeing the code that queries them.

annotations.body: Using TEXT for password_hash is fine — bcrypt hashes are always 60 chars, but TEXT avoids migration if we switch algorithms.

sections.heading: TypeScript types: User, UserRole, UserSession
sections.rationale: The type definitions mirror the DB schema and define the API contract.

sections.heading: Core auth logic: createUser and login
sections.rationale: The main business logic — creating users and authenticating them.

annotations.body: Good — checking for existing users before insert prevents duplicate accounts.
Both missing-user and wrong-password return the same error message. This prevents user enumeration attacks.

sections.heading: Auth middleware: requireAuth and requireRole
sections.rationale: The middleware enforces auth on protected routes — this is where the session token is validated.

annotations.body: Note: `findByToken` is synchronous here but `findById` in `requireRole` is async. Is the sessions lookup cached?

sections.heading: Route wiring: new endpoints added
sections.rationale: Finally, see how the new auth endpoints are mounted alongside existing routes.
